name: Carotid endarterectomy

figure_scopes.storyboard: Bifurcation/stenosis -> clamp/arteriotomy -> plaque removal plane -> patch/restored lumen
figure_scopes.closeup: Plaque at the bifurcation/ICA origin within the lumen

laterality: Neck; internal carotid artery posterolateral with no neck branches, external carotid artery gives branches, bifurcation central
must_have: Common carotid artery, internal carotid artery, external carotid artery, carotid bifurcation, plaque/stenosis zone, vagus nerve region, and hypoglossal nerve if superior exposure is shown.
Arteriotomy/patch concept only if requested and non-gory.
spatial_rules: Internal carotid artery is posterior/lateral and has no neck branches in the illustrated segment.
External carotid artery gives branches and should not be mislabeled as ICA.
Carotid bifurcation should be central to the stenosis/plaque teaching view.

forbidden_errors.error: Internal and external carotid arteries swapped.
Branches added to the internal carotid artery in the neck.
Plaque placed outside the carotid lumen or as an unrelated mass.

safety_concept: Correctly distinguish ICA (no neck branches) from ECA; keep plaque intraluminal at the bifurcation/ICA origin.
phases: Plaque removal plane or lumen restoration concept.
Patch/closed artery with restored flow, if requested.
verify: ICA/ECA identities and branch pattern are correct.
Plaque location is intraluminal at the bifurcation/ICA origin as intended.
Nerve landmarks are not confused with vessels.
